title: Origin Stories on my Website Tour - Stoned

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a tour of the origin stories section of his website, explaining how he has organized 47 origin stories from 13 countries into a text-heavy layout optimized for text-to-speech computer reading. He discusses the scale of the content (24,528 pages), his chapter/column menu system, and his use of Flickr for images.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Everyone should memorize world origin stories; the human brain is capable of it and it's worth the effort
His design is the best in the world for computer-assisted reading due to maximizing text per page
Flickr is a good hosting solution because it doesn't slow down page load times

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have six different sections of the word of the world for origin stories right now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: We should all have all of the world's origin stories memorized because the stories are very interesting.

claims.subject: human brain
claims.claim: It is possible for the human brain to remember pretty much all of the events in the story so we will know the entire history of the world from the time.

claims.subject: world
claims.claim: Every 500 miles people looked different, racially and had completely different customs and languages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I currently have 47 origin stories from 13 countries containing 24,528 pages of text.

claims.subject: reading time
claims.claim: At 50 seconds per page, which is a rate that max read at double speed, that is 341 hours or 14.2 days or 28.4 days of continuous reading.

claims.subject: Kyle's website
claims.claim: The way that I have my origin stories organized on my website is the best design, the world over if you want to have the computer read to you.

claims.subject: Kyle's website
claims.claim: I have the largest amount of text on one page so you can high-type the whole page and have it meet you for an hour instead of having to click to the next page and hit text every 30 seconds.

claims.subject: memorization
claims.claim: In your life you can memorize this stuff man, it's possible.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have 47 origin stories from 13 countries.

claims.subject: computer reading
claims.claim: You can have the computer read to you, for example, the Mahabharata.

claims.subject: Kyle's books
claims.claim: I'm going to have a page pictures first for all my books when I get around to it.

claims.subject: Kyle's setup
claims.claim: I like the setup I have with audio books, it's really good for history books because you can put the maps on either side of what you're talking about.

claims.subject: Flickr
claims.claim: Flickr has cool pictures to use and they don't make the page slower to load because they're hosted on another site.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to collect the most important origin stories, the biggest ones that are the basic ones of all the world.

claims.subject: Kyle's organization
claims.claim: I organize my origin stories with a five column menu list.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My favorite origin story is her out of Tissis Mysteries.

claims.subject: Kyle's system
claims.claim: If you wanted to make your own audio files, you could copy and paste pages to make your own using my system.
